title: Login — CLIENT happy path: valid credentials navigate to /(client)/home

preconditions.description: Expo web app is running and reachable at the configured base URL.
Backend is running with a working POST /auth/login endpoint.
A verified CLIENT account exists with email DATA-1 and password DATA-2. Can be created via API setup in beforeAll.

The CLIENT user has completed onboarding (onboarding localStorage key is set) OR the test clears onboarding state separately. Without this, the layout guard will redirect to onboarding instead of home.
preconditions.note: Set localStorage key 'companion_onboarding_complete' to 'true' before navigating, or ensure the CLIENT onboarding flow is tested separately (FE-M02-ONBOARDING-001).

testData.name: clientEmail
testData.description: Email of a pre-verified CLIENT account.

testData.name: clientPassword
testData.description: Password of the pre-verified CLIENT account.

journey.description: Confirm login screen heading is visible.
Wait for navigation to client home (may pass through onboarding redirect if not suppressed).

assertions.description: App navigates to CLIENT home screen.
CLIENT home screen content is visible.

manualValidationRequired.reason: No supported native automation runner is currently configured.
No supported native automation runner is currently configured.

notes: PRE-4 is critical: the /(client)/_layout.tsx checks hasCompletedOnboarding() and redirects to /(client)/onboarding if false. The test must either set localStorage 'companion_onboarding_complete' = 'true' before login, or accept the redirect and navigate through onboarding first.
The validator should inject localStorage via page.evaluate() in the test setup if skipping onboarding is desired.
The GET /users/me call for session restore may also fire on app load — the test starts from /(auth)/login to bypass this.
